Of all the ways you can educate, build relationships, and get your customers to spend
more money, email marketing is arguably the ROI King. Email earns an estimated
$42 for every $1 spent!
While email marketing has taken its share of criticism over the years, it is safe to say
that email is alive and well. In fact, 9 out of 10 marketers rate email as strategically
important to their day-to-day customer communications. Chances are you’re a
subscriber to several company email lists yourself (including ours!)
While most companies see value in email, most fail to get results. That is why this
manual is filled with email marketing tips, tactics, and strategies so you can...

-- 4 of 37 --

One of the most important metrics for any company in today’s market is the size and
growth of its email list. It is highly recommended that every campaign you run in some
way generates an email lead. A common way to do that is with the use of landing pages
and sales funnels, which you can create using Clickfunnels (10xresource.com/clickfunnels).
What is unique about email versus other types of marketing is that it is permission-based.
That means that you can only market to those people who have subscribed and they can
unsubscribe at any time. For this reason, you must always think about your list as real
people and work hard to cultivate a relationship. We’ll teach you how!
If you’re email marketing effectively you can make about
$1 per month for every email subscriber on your list.
If you’re doing it my way, you can potentially 10X that!
5
RECOMMENDED TOOL
HUBSPOT

at 10X we use HubSpot. For that reason, we will reference it in numerous
places throughout the manual. You can find additional options for email
providers in a later section. HubSpot is considered an advanced solution for
those who are looking for a complete CRM.

RECOMMENDED TOOL
MAILCHIMP

likely need something more basic and inexpensive. With Mailchimp, you
can get started with a free account. MailChimp is a great marketing
platform, especially for startups and small businesses.

6
EMAIL MARKETING TERMINOLOGY
Here’s a quick reference guide with some of the terms and phrases you’ll find in this
manual and elsewhere.
This is the rate at where your emails were NOT delivered to your targets/leads. The reasons
for bounce rate can include non-existent, invalid, and blocked email addresses, a busy
email server, or a full recipient inbox.
BOUNCE RATE
The Controlling the Assault of Non-Solicited Pornography And Marketing Act is a 2003 law
that sets the rules, requirements, and regulations for commercial email. It also gives email
recipients the option and right to stop receiving future emails from a company upon
request and indicates the penalties for violations.
CAN-SPAM ACT
Customer Relationship Management is software that manages all your customer and
email subscriber’s contact information, identify sales opportunities, record service issues,
and manage marketing campaigns, all in one central location.
CRM
The number of people who clicked on a link inside your email, divided by the number of
people who opened the email. This can also be referred to as click-to-open rate on some
platforms.
CLICKTHROUGH RATE
The number of people who clicked on a link inside your email, divided by the number of
emails you sent.
CLICK RATE
1000 Emails
Sent
200 Emails
Opened
20% Open Rate
20 Emails Clicked
10% Clickthrough Rate
(2% Click Rate)
An illustration of the most important email statistics
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 37 --

The measure of people who completed a predefined action; such as a successful product
purchase or registration to a webinar, divided by the total number of visitors.
CONVERSION RATE
The practice of requiring subscribers to click on a confirmation link via email before the
user is officially added to the email marketing list.
DOUBLE OPT-IN
It is the most valuable asset in Email Marketing. Your Email List contains information
about all the people who have opted-in to receive email marketing from your company.
EMAIL LIST
A lead-capture page on your site that is linked to an email to provide additional
information on your products or services promoted in the email's call-to-action link.
LANDING PAGE
It is a marketing term for a free item or service that is offered to a potential subscriber
who exchanges their information to gain access to item(s) in the offer.
LEAD MAGNET
The process of dividing up an email list into smaller groups of contacts that share the
same characteristics. Examples are, different demographics, use cases, industries,
location, and so forth. Using segmentation improves email marketing performance.
LIST SEGMENTATION
The percentage of subscribers who open specific emails divided by the total number of
emails sent to all subscribers.
OPEN RATE
This is an online form where users enter and submit information.
OPT-IN FORM

-- 7 of 37 --

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
When a person gives their email address or contact information to a company so they can
receive email marketing messages from that company in the future.
OPT-IN (OR SUBSCRIBE)
When a subscriber chooses to stop receiving email communications from a company.
A subscriber can process this usually by clicking the required “unsubscribe” link at the
bottom of any email.
OPT-OUT (OR UNSUBSCRIBE)
A text-only version of an email that is sent without the use of images, links, graphics,
HTML code, or formatting.
PLAIN TEXT EMAIL
When a new email address is added to your mailing list WITHOUT being required to
confirm their email addresses after choosing to opt-in and receive your email marketing.
SINGLE OPT-IN
Emails sent to a recipient who has not given their consent to receive messages from the
sender. Typically, spam is sent for commercial purposes.
SPAM
A person who has shared their email address and contact information with a company,
and has agreed for that company to send them marketing messages via email.
SUBSCRIBER
Action taken by a person who chooses to stop receiving any further email messages from
a company. This is generally done with a link at the bottom of an email.
UNSUBSCRIBE
8
Opt-out links are provided standardly by all email providers

9
TYPES OF EMAIL MARKETING
BROADCASTS VS. AUTORESPONDERS
All emails are sent in one of 2 ways: as a broadcast or as an automated email (often
referred to as an “autoresponder”).
BROADCAST EMAILS
Broadcasts are the individual emails that you will send to your subscribers. When
planned in advance, a series of emails may be sent as part of a larger campaign,
which we will discuss in more detail later.
Broadcast emails can be sent to your entire email list or to a specific segment.
Segmentation will be covered in more detail later, but is generally performed by
creating lists and also by excluding certain groups of people. We’re also going to
discuss best practices including how to optimize every email you send.
EXAMPLES OF WHEN TO USE BROADCASTS...
Making important announcements or sending newsletters
You need to send an email on a specific date and time
Promotions for one-time offers or events
Insert example of a broadcast list setup in Hubspot

As a company that focuses heavily on promotions, we
make more money from broadcast emails than from any
other channel. It is the #1 way we monetize our audience.
Generally speaking, sending broadcasts is how most day-to-day email marketing is
done. It is relatively simple to send a broadcast and most tools will also allow you to
schedule a broadcast in advance.

AUTOMATED EMAILS
The second main type of emails are
autoresponders, which are essentially a
series of emails sent on auto-pilot. A series
of autoresponder emails is usually referred
to as a “sequence”.
At its most basic level, automated email
sequences consist of individual emails,
separated by timers. These timers are
generally a specific amount of delay (i.e.
wait 48 hours before sending) but they can
also be triggered to send on certain days of
the week, etc.

HOW EMAIL AUTOMATION WORKS
EXAMPLES OF WHEN TO USE BROADCASTS...
A customers purchases a product and needs welcomed
Someone subscribed to receive a free digital download

11
One of the big mistakes most marketers make when building their email lists is
they fail to follow up with new subscribers. Autoresponders give you the ability to
build real relationships and to customize the experience that your subscribers have,
at any point in their journey.
Every type of email marketer will have a different automation setup process. Any
quality email marketer will provide drag and drop functionality for building your
automated sequences. These systems allow you to create what is usually referred to as
“workflows”. You saw an example of a simple workflow on the previous page.
It is worth noting that while the features, interface and terminology will vary on various
email providers, the premise remains the same...
While automated emails do require some additional setup, most email
marketing systems (including Hubspot and Mailchimp) can be done
relatively easily with no technical skills needed.
ADDITIONAL RESOURCES:
• Hubspot Automation Tutorial (10xresource.com/em5)
• Mailchimp Automation Tutorial (10xresource.com/em10)
Autoresponder sequences are started by a specific action or
behavior (usually referred to as a “trigger”)
Autoresponder sequences are also stopped by a specific
action or behavior (usually referred to as a “goal”)

AUTOMATED SEQUENCE TYPES
12
The user actions that can start or stop automated sequences can vary widely…
Once you understand the basics of email automation, you can start to build sequences
and even tie those sequences together. The opportunities are limitless. We’ll discuss
some example types of sequences in the next section, and then how to tie them
together in the “email journey” section.
While you can set up automated email sequences for any purpose, here are some of
the more common types that we recommend. Keep in mind these are all examples,
the specifics of your business may differ...
Initial welcome emails that warm your prospect up to do business with you.
You’ll want to provide social proof, share stories, provide free resources and do
anything you can to start the relationship off right.
Start Trigger: New subscriber
Duration: Typically 3-5 emails
End Goal: Buy or abandon cart
Swipe File: 10xdownload.com/em7
Form signup
Email opened
Link clicked
Tag added
Field value change
Added to list
Product purchased
Abandoned cart
Time limit passed
Specific dates
Don’t be intimidated by automation, they’ve made the
technology simple these days. More importantly, once set
up, it can make you money while you sleep… forever.
Remember that people are subscribed to a LOT of email
lists. If you don’t reach out to a new subscriber early and
often they will forget who you are. Don’t let that happen.
INDOCTRINATION SEQUENCE	1
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 37 --

13
This sequence is where you introduce your offers to your subscriber lists. You’ll
have at least one sequence for each product or service. Regardless of the
content, the result is generating a sales lead or selling a product.
Start Trigger: Opt-in or end indoctrination
Duration: As long as it takes to sell!
End Goal: Buy product or create appointment
Swipe File: 10xdownload.com/em8
SALES SEQUENCE	2
In many situations, you’ll want to have a unique set of emails that are sent to
close the deal after a user has shown interest. For example, if someone registers
for a webinar or applies for your high-end services.
Start Trigger: Complete form or click a link
Duration: Typically 3-7 emails
End Goal: Buy product or service
Swipe File: 10xdownload.com/em12
FOLLOW-UP SEQUENCE	3
This is the series of emails sent to someone after they purchase. The first
objective is to provide needed product information and make them feel good.
That said, don’t be afraid to upsell. Buyers will always buy more!
Start Trigger: Complete form or click a link
Duration: Typically 2-5 emails
End Goal: Goes until sequence ends
Swipe File: 10xdownload.com/em9
BUYER SEQUENCE	4
Depending on what you sell, it is highly recommended that you break your
checkout into multiple steps. This allows you to capture people who start to buy
and don’t finish, allowing for additional sales follow-up.
Start Trigger: Start checkout process
Duration: Typically 2-3 emails
End Goal: Buy product or service
Swipe File: 10xdownload.com/em10
ABANDONER SEQUENCE	5

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
If someone starts purchasing, assume they still want to
buy. Distractions are everywhere, so you’re doing them
a favor by reminding them to complete their order.
There are certain emails that do not require opt-in or the ability to opt-out, which are
called “transactional emails”. These are individual emails sent to one person at a time
(not large broadcasts) for a particular transactional purpose. Examples of this include
invoices for payment, receipts of purchase, confirmation of registration, etc.
Oftentimes transactional emails will be sent by systems other than your email marketing
provider. For example, Clickfunnels (10xresource.com/clickfunnels) can send receipt of
purchase emails. More advanced email systems such as Hubspot will allow you to send
messages either designated as marketing or transactional.
NOTE: If you do business in the European Union you’ll want to familiarize yourself with
GDPR regulations as well (10xresource.com/em29).
The CAN-SPAM Act protects consumers and makes it a bad idea
to use email for evil. Since modern email providers handle the
technical compliance for you, as long as you don’t make false
promises or scam people, you’ll be fine. That said, you should be
aware of the FTC guidelines (10xresource.com/em28).
ADDITIONAL RESOURCES:
• Hubspot Automation Tutorial (10xresource.com/em5)
• Mailchimp Automation Tutorial (10xresource.com/em10)
14
TRANSACTIONAL EMAILS AND SPAM
The majority of emails we have discussed thus far would be considered “marketing
emails”, therefore requiring an opt-in and the ability to opt-out. Whenever you are
using email for promotional purposes, even if not directly selling a product, you
always need to follow applicable rules and regulations.

15
GETTING STARTED WITH
EMAIL MARKETING
EMAIL SERVICE PROVIDERS VS. CRMS
The first major decision you’ll need to make is what system to use to manage your
email marketing. Beyond email, it is important to understand that this system will
likely serve as your centralized customer database. Everything you do will be tied to
your email system, as later discussed in the integration section.
Generally speaking, your options for service providers fall into one of these two
categories:
ESPs are generally lower-priced services that are slightly simpler to use. While they
often do provide additional features (some provide text messaging,) they are
primarily focused on email. ESPs are usually used by startups and small businesses.
CRMs generally cost more but provide more capability than an ESP. CRMs
manage the customer life cycle from beginning to end. They can be used to
manage a company’s marketing teams, sales teams, and support teams all with
one system. While CRMs are used by companies of all sizes, they may be a bit
much for a startup.
CUSTOMER RELATIONSHIP MANAGERS (CRM)
EMAIL SERVICE PROVIDERS (ESP)
Mailchimp

Aweber

ConstantContact

SAP
Oracle

-- 15 of 37 --

16
LEAD GENERATION AND FORMS
It should be noted that as ESPs become more and more robust, the line separating
a CRM from an ESP becomes more and more blurred.
How do you decide which system is right for you?
It comes down to what features you need and how big your budget is. Systems
have very different pricing models based on list size, emails sent, features provided,
etc. Beyond that, if you don’t have a team of people to help you, you may want to
keep it simple by avoiding more advanced CRM systems. At the end of the day,
every business is different.
It goes without saying that a very important step in this process is to make sure
leads are being sent into your email system.
Unless added manually, emails will always originate from a form. While all ESPs and
CRMs will provide forms that you can add to your websites or funnels, often this will
not be needed. Whenever possible, you’ll want to utilize integrations that allow you
to connect to systems without code or technical assistance.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
While some care should go into choosing a system, don’t
let it slow you down. Any of them will get the job done
and you do have the ability to switch later if you want.

Typeform for advanced forms and surveys

17
LEAD GENERATION AND FORMS
If you are using a website or funnel system that does not integrate with your
email provider, then you’ll need to use native webforms. Generally this will
provide pasting a piece of code onto your web pages. This may require
assistance from a developer.
Segmentation is the practice of adding your subscribers to specific lists based on
their actions, history or interests. You can create as many lists as you wish and
subscribers can be included on any number of lists at any given time.
The reason for segmentation is to provide your subscriber with a more customized
experience and ultimately to maximize your ability to monetize. Simply put, the
better your segmentation, the more personalized your messaging, the higher the
conversions. You can use your lists to either include or exclude certain groups of
people from both automated emails and broadcasts.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Here are a few examples of potential segmentation criteria...
Activity such as clicks, opens or purchases
Customer lifecycle stage (lead, buyer, etc)
Overall lifetime value of a paid customer
Answers to surveys (interests, industry, etc)
Geographical location or other demographics
ADDITIONAL RESOURCES:
• Hubspot Webforms (10xresource.com/em25)
• Mailchimp Webforms (10xresource.com/em26)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 37 --

INCENTIVES AND LEAD MAGNETS
While the goal is to have as many paid customers on your email list as possible,
the reality is that most subscribers won’t pull out their wallets immediately. If you
plan to grow your list, it is essential that you create some sort of compelling reason
for people to subscribe.
Traditionally, this was done by letting people sign up for an email “newsletter”.
While a quality newsletter can be valuable, especially when it is done consistently
and with great content, it is not always the best incentive for someone to give you
their email address. In order to better attract subscribers, it is recommended that
you provide something in exchange. This is usually referred to as a “lead magnet”.
While lead magnets are often some sort of digital download, they don’t have to
be. For example, a “sales call” can be easily reframed as a “free custom strategy
session”. Regardless of the format, the important thing to remember about your
lead magnet is that in addition to helping generate the lead, it should also help
create an eventual sale.
Don’t overcomplicate segmentation at first. Start with a
few major buckets (general subscribers, buyers, event
attendees, etc) and then get more targeted as you grow.
We live in an age where information is free and abundant.
You need to create a lead magnet that is so compelling a
prospect is happy to trade their email in return.
18
Having this sort of information will not only improve your ability to convert,
it will help make sure you don’t put the wrong messages in front of people. For
example, we send a very high volume of emails, but most emails we send only go
out to our most engaged list (i.e. people who have recently opened and clicked).
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.

-- 18 of 37 --

CREATING A CUSTOMER AVATAR
If you haven’t figured it out yet, there is a lot that goes into doing email marketing
the right way. It is important to remember that above all else, no matter how
many people are on your list, your messages are being read by individual human
beings. At the end of the day, if you don’t know who you’re talking to, none of the
rest of it really matters.
19
EXAMPLES OF LEAD MAGNETS:
Workbook lead magnet for the 10X Income Webclass
eBooks and reports
Checklists
Mini-course
Training or webinars
Gift cards or discounts
Starter kit bundles
Free trial
7-day training series
Quizzes
Contests
Never write an email for an audience. Always imagine that
any message you create is being written directly to one
individual person. You will never appease everyone.
A widely accepted marketing practice is to identify your ideal customer (also
known as an “avatar”). This is an exercise that goes far beyond knowing simple
demographics or stats about your subscribers. You will want to actually create one
or more character personas, even giving your avatars real names!
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 37 --

20
What is their age and gender?
What is most important to them?
What are they struggling with?
What do they spend money on?
How do they make buying decisions?
How would they find your company?
What would they like about your product?
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Obviously, having an existing customer base that provides feedback can make it
easier to know who your audience really is. That said, this doesn’t need to be
rocket science. You can always create new avatars as your company grows. What
matters is that writing emails specifically for “Bob” or “Mary” is far more effective
than creating generic messages for a crowd.
Here are some potential questions to answer when creating an avatar…
Free tool to help you build avatars (10xresource.com/em16)

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
21
FINDING YOUR VOICE
In the same way it is essential that you write TO a specific individual, it is just as
important that you write FROM a specific individual as well. In other words, sending
emails from a company will never be as effective as writing emails from an actual person.
Whether you are writing emails for your own company or writing in someone else’s voice,
it is essential that the author be clearly defined and developed. A simple way to begin
this process is to create a list of characteristics you wish to convey. Here is an example list
of characteristics that could be used to describe Grant Cardone:
Email marketing is all about creating a real connection. The deeper the relationship,
the higher your open rates, click-through rates and sales will be.
In addition to revealing personality, you will also want to make sure that throughout
your emails you reveal the backstory of the author. Specifically, the challenges that
you have faced, what was the moment of breakthrough, and what was the result of
such a triumph. This is an important exercise that you will often hear referred to as
the “hero’s journey”
Don’t stress about writing emails, they don’t need to be
perfect. In fact, it is better to be real and raw than overly
refined. “You be you” and the right people will respond.
GRANT
CARDONE
is…
Direct
Motivational
Relentless
Affluent
Charitable
Hard-working
Strong
Charismatic
Unapologetic
Experienced
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 37 --

CREATING SUBJECT LINES
Email success all starts with the reader opening the email. For that reason, writing
compelling subject lines is the most important skill in email marketing.
Needless to say, a subject line needs to be attention-grabbing. After all, the average user
receives well over 100+ emails per day (business people receive way more). So what
makes a subject line effective? When you boil it all down, people open emails for
3 primary reasons…
While any of these motivators can be compelling enough to generate great open rates,
there is one that stands out above the rest. In fact, when you master the ability to evoke
this one emotion, it can be a complete gamechanger. Which is it? We’ll tell you at the
bottom of the next page.*
22
EMAIL AND COPYWRITING
ESSENTIALS
A.
B.
C.
Excitement for an Offered Benefit
Fear of Missing Out on Something
Curiosity About What Is Inside

I want to make your job easier (Name)
Steal my secrets for increasing sales daily
Free Download
STEP 1

-- 22 of 37 --

As you can see, there is no exact formula for what will perform the best. With that said,
here are some tips that will help...
Use personalization
All email marketers allow you to dynamically insert information such as first name.
As you can see, we’ve also utilized others including email address and city.
Occasional CAPS
While too many caps are annoying and spammy, when used correctly, you can
emphasize specific words to increase the overall impact.
Ask smart questions
When you ask questions that seem to require an answer it can be effective. When you
ask rhetorical questions, don’t expect much.
Use random numbers
Numbers stick out from all the other letters in people’s inboxes. If you can use super
specific and random numbers, that’s even better.
Use emoji and symbols
Adding emojis can grab the eye, just be sure to do it sparingly. 	Also, note that using
too many!!!!! can also seem spammy`
*Your business name here*
???
Build a profitable business that grows by itself
There's something holding back your business
Set your goal...
It's Grant and I want to help you build a
business right now
How I made $5.5 million in 90 days
Stop what you are doing, I have
something for you
23
IMPORTANT NOTE ABOUT MOBILE:
Over 80% of emails are now read on a mobile device. Keep this in mind
with all aspects of email, but especially with subject lines. A typical
mobile device will only display the first 30 or so characters of an email
subject line, so make the first words count!
Most fail by THINKING they have it
figured out when they don't
You need a 10X Business Plan (Name)
Thanks (first name)
10X merch, huge discount (all yours inside)
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 37 --

Super short subjects
Sometimes less is more, and having a short subject can actually make you stick out. Like
with everything else, mix this in occasionally.
Use a current event
If you’re able to somehow reference something that is top of people’s minds it can grab
people. Also, holidays are always a hit.
Include golden words
Research has shown that people respond to words that they like hearing such as please,
thanks, congratulations, and free.
Avoid spam phrases
Things like “guaranteed”, “sign up now”, “millions”, “make money”, “lose weight” can hurt
deliverability. We’ll discuss this more in a bit.
* So what’s the #1 secret to getting people to open your emails? Create curiosity. In fact,
your curiosity probably made you scroll down here just to see the answer.
Email copywriting is one of those things that can take years to master. It is as much an
art as it is a science. With that said, realize that your emails do not need to be
masterpieces. You’ll get better with time and sending anything is far better than sending
nothing.
An effective email only needs 3 parts...
24
Bob’s Bakery
Would you like some bread and butt…
There is no more yummy way to start…
Jul 19
WRITING EFFECTIVE EMAILS
PART 1 	IMMEDIATE HOOK 	PART 2 	BODY COPY 	PART 3 	LEAR CALL TO ACTION

-- 24 of 37 --

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
You’ve got about 5 seconds to help the reader answer the
question “why should I read this email?” You’ve got to
sell them on reading before you sell them on clicking.
Attention spans are short, especially on mobile devices. While it is always good to start
with a quick greeting, your first few lines need to grab the reader.
The hard reality is that most people don’t really care too much about you or your
company. They care about what is in it for them. If you can make that clear immediately
and it seems like it is worth their time, they will read on.
When writing your emails, your primary goal is usually to get a click. It’s not to sell a
product or service, it’s simply to get them to take the next step. That said, never lose track
of the bigger picture either. If the reader gets the feeling that they are nothing more
than a click to you, then they are far less likely to open future emails.
25
PART 1 	IMMEDIATE HOOK
PART 2 	BODY COPY
Hey (first name)! I thought this might help you get more customers in your store...
Fair warning, what you’re about to read may shock you.
I wanted to offer you our biggest discount of all time (first name)
I just lost $29,331. I want to make sure you avoid making the same mistake.
People have been begging me for this, but I’m only sending it to my most
loyal readers (yes, that includes you (first name)!)
If you are still looking to flatten your abs, then this is going to save you
a LOT of time.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 37 --

Remember that you’ll never achieve all these things in one email, nor do you need
to. While we want to provide you with insights to help you succeed, there is no
substitute for experience. Just start writing and you’ll probably surprise yourself!
Adding a subscriber’s first name in the subject line and body copy has been
shown to boost open and clicks by as much as 20%. That said, if you don’t regularly
collect first name on your opt-in forms, you should definitely avoid it.
26
THINGS TO INCLUDE:
Humor - Realize you don’t need to be
laugh-out-loud funny, but even a
simple pun can go a long way.
Personality - Let loose and
experiment. It is better to have
someone not like it than to not care.
PS at Bottom - Besides the first
words, the next most read part of your
email is the end. PPS: Try multiple!
Real Urgency - People need a kick in
the butt. If they don’t do it now, then
they aren’t likely to do it at all.
Creativity - And it’s not just the words
you write. Play with the formatting
and styling of the words as well.
THINGS TO AVOID:
Fake Enthusiasm - There is no bigger
turnoff to readers than unneeded and
insincere excitement!!!!!
Predictability - For example, don’t
always send long emails and short
sales emails. Be sure to mix it up!
Monotony - If you structure all your
text and sentences exactly the same
way, it will get B-O-R-I-N-G!
Fake Urgency - If there is always a
deadline, or you don’t give a real
reason, your reader won’t buy it.
Spam Words - While some are
unavoidable, minimize whenever
possible. We will discuss more.
NOTE ABOUT NAME PERSONALIZATION

EMAIL TEMPLATE DESIGN
27
While you won’t usually give your full CTA until the end,
that doesn’t mean you shouldn’t try to get clicks throughout
the body of the email. More links means more clicks.
99% of our emails are simple text with one image at most.
For that reason, when we do create fancier email designs,
they usually perform really well. It’s good to mix it up.
Regarding the actual link itself, you will most frequently use hyperlinks (which are
simply words you’ve turned into a link). Much like with subject lines, leaving a little
mystery can often lead to getting more clicks. For example...
Learn more and see time options will beat Register for the 1:00 training here
In email marketing, your template is the standard design that you will use over
and over.
The first thing to understand about email design the 10X way is that if you want to
maximize your results, it’s not about making “pretty” emails. While there are
certainly situations where designing more image-heavy emails can make sense
(for example: high end-newsletters and fashionable eCommerce products) most
of the time you want to keep it simple.
The biggest mistake you can make is not making it super easy for readers to know
what to do. Toward the end of your email you will always want to give a compelling
reason why the user should click a link and go to your desired web page.
Remember, your job in the email is to get the click. In the situation where you are
sending people to a sales page, you are generally better to let the sales page do most
of the selling.
PART 3 	CLEAR CALL TO ACTION	www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 27 of 37 --

The first reason for this is to ensure your emails hit the inbox. The more images you add,
the more likely your message will go to spam or be marked as “promotional” in gmail.
(we’ll talk more about this later).
The other reason is that you don’t want to spend a lot
of time and energy worrying about graphics.
Everything you design will need to look good on
both mobile and desktop. It’s a lot. Instead, focus on
creating messages that convert and getting your
messages in front of more people.
If you are in an industry that would benefit from a more robust email design, then most
email providers will give you nicer templates to work from. You can also get inspiration
from big brands by going to Milled.com where you can search company newsletters.
With that said, always be sure you watch your stats. It’s not about looking good, it’s
about getting results!
The golden rule is to include no more than ONE image and you’ll be fine.
RECOMMENDED TEMPLATE SPECS:
Width: 600 pixels
Font Type: Arial
Font Size: 15 pt.
Font Color: Black
Link Color: Blue
This is about as fancy as we get!
Want to design a nicer template design?
Avoid big blocks of text - They make it hard to read, so break it up
Use bold and italics - People skim, so highlight important phrases
Add buttons at the bottom - This will almost always increase clicks
Quick tips for overall design…

-- 28 of 37 --

CREATING A BROADCAST CALENDAR
Your broadcast calendar is a staple for your business. Creating your schedule should
become a regular ritual, even when you have a small email list. Ideally, you will map out
your calendar once a year, planning out all seasonal and annual promotions, then once
per month you’ll fill in any flexible dates.
The key to successful email marketing is to think about it in terms of campaigns.
A campaign can last anywhere between 3 to 30 days, depending on what you are
promoting or trying to achieve.
When sending emails in a pre-planned campaign you have the ability to build
excitement. You also are able to provide foreshadowing about what is to come, which
can help increase your open rates for subsequent emails. You do this by including
“open loops”. Examples could be as simple as “you’re not going to believe what I share
with you tomorrow” or “we are closing this down Sunday at midnight, so be watching
your inbox”.
29
PLANNING AND IMPLEMENTATION
You should always begin with the end in mind. When
planning your promotional calendar, start with your
income target and then plan out how to achieve the goal.
Annual Planning Examples
• Holiday Promotion
• Promos for Slow Months
• Product Launches
• Company Events
Annual Planning Examples
• Product Promotions
• Webinars/Livestreams
• Value and Content
• Media Appearance

-- 29 of 37 --

Planning your promotions and campaigns can be relatively simple. We generally use a
whiteboard or paper to visually sketch how things will flow. Get creative!
Once your campaigns are mapped out, it’s time to get to work on writing your emails.
We recommend creating your copy in Google Documents. It saves your work as you go,
and allows for easy editing and sharing.
In addition to the main body copy of your messages, it is helpful to include the other
information needed to send a broadcast...
Most people won’t open your emails, but almost everyone
will read your subject lines. If you have important info
such as a close down date, include it in your subject.
EXAMPLE PLANNING INFO:
Date and time: Aug 12th at 1:00 EST
Email topic: open mentoring application
Subject line: Can I help (first name)?
Email preview: If you’re feeling a little stuck,
we can get you back on track (first name)....
List(s) to send to: All non-mentoring students
Link: 10xmentorprogram.com/apply
Sent from name: Grant Cardone
Reply to email: info@grantcardone.com
Example Flow of an Email Campaign...
BEGINNING EMAILS
Peak Interest
(less detail)
MIDDLE EMAILS
Share Benefits
(more detail)
ENDING EMAILS
Create Urgency
(shut down)
Adjust your sales angle and messaging throughout the campaign

From the moment someone subscribes to your email list, your job is to provide them the
most ideal experience possible. Email marketing is a relationship, and the stronger the
relationship you build, the more money you will ultimately make. To achieve this, it is
important to think about each subscriber as an individual on their own journey.
Your customer’s email journey is usually created by combining various automated email
sequences. While the possibilities are limitless, you’ll often use the 5 sequence types we
discussed earlier:
While this may seem a bit complex, once you start to build out automated sequences,
you’ll better understand how the pieces work together. An important thing to
understand is that within your workflows/journeys you will often want to label your
subscribers to keep track of who has seen what emails. In Mailchimp this is done using
tags, in Hubspot it is done using

-- 31 of 37 --

Start by keeping it simple, but remember that the better
you monetize with your email list, the more money you
can spend on ads. This is what leads to explosive growth.
Once your ideal customer journey has been created using automated emails, the next
thing to take into consideration is how your broadcast emails will come into play. There
will be times where you will want to exclude people who are in certain automated email
sequences from receiving your broadcasts. For example, it can sometimes cause
confusion if a user is sent 2 different sales promotions at the same time.

© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
“REPLY BACK” EMAIL STRATEGY
This strategy is ridiculously simple and ridiculously effective. In fact, it requires no work
and can work for any type of business.
The hack is this: rather than sending email with the goal of getting people to click and go
to a web page, instead get them to reply back to your email. This will get a real
conversation going, and make it way more likely for you to turn into a customer.
Here’s an example (the simpler the better)...
Obviously, the approach you take to this will vary widely depending on your business.
This is a very natural tactic for service-based companies or high-end products. That said,
even if you sell low-priced items online, it can still lead to more sales. Try offering a
coupon code or any other idea you can think of to get people engaged and interested.
By getting people to reply to your emails it will also improve deliverability!
SUBJECT: Hey!
Hey [first name], hope you’re doing well!
I’m curious, are you still wanting to [insert desired outcome]?
If so, just reply back and let me know where you’re at and how I can help.
If you prefer, you can also call my office at [phone number].
Chat soon!
[Your Name]
33
TIPS, TRICKS AND HACKS

USING VIDEO IMAGE THUMBNAILS
If you are sending people to a page with a video, this is a trick you should use every time!
As you may know, it is not physically possible to send videos inside of an email. That said,
it is very possible to use the video in order to get more click-throughs to your desired
landing page.
34
Example of a video thumbnail for email
Here’s how you can do this yourself...
If you’re not sure how to do a screen capture this article will help…
10xresource.com/em33
STEP 1 	Take a screen capture of the video to turn it into an image.
This free tool makes it quick and easy to do… 10xresource.com/em34
STEP 2 	Add a play button to the image if it doesn’t have one.
This step will be slightly different on each email provider, but it is easy to do.
STEP 3 	Add the image to your email and make it a hyperlink.

ADDITIONAL EMAILS FOR CLICKERS
This is a more advanced technique, but it can be well worth the extra effort as your email
list gets bigger. Using this method will allow you to better engage with active subscribers,
without having to bother the inactive ones. It is achieved by creating a series of automated
emails which are sent to people who either open OR click on a broadcast email.
Here are the basics of how you set it up...
35
In marketing, think of new ways to leverage your most
active people to help multiply results. Openers will always
open, clickers will always click, and buyers will always buy.
You will do this the way that you regularly do. Either schedule the broadcast for a
later time, or save it as a draft to send later.
STEP 1 	Set up your broadcast email, but don’t send it immediately.
STEP 3 	Send the broadcast email as you normally would
After you’ve set up the journey/workflow to be triggered by a click or open of the
broadcast, you’re good to send the broadcast. Note that each email provider will be
set up differently, but the principle is the same.
STEP 2 	Set up a workflow/journey that includes additional emails for clickers.
You will set the trigger to
either be a click or an open
of a broadcast email.
Always start the
journey/workflow with a
delay timer. So for
example, you might want
to send the first email one
hour after the broadcast
email is clicked. The
automated sequence will
likely only be a few emails
When creating a journey in Mailchimp choose this setting
